verses.text: Jeremiah begins his prophecies in Josiah's days and through Jehoiakim and Zedekiah, until the captivity. His ministry spans decades of decline. God set a faithful voice through the long, sad slide of Judah to exile.

Jeremiah begins his prophecies in Josiah's days and through Jehoiakim and Zedekiah, until the captivity. His ministry spans decades of decline. God set a faithful voice through the long, sad slide of Judah to exile.

Jeremiah begins his prophecies in Josiah's days and through Jehoiakim and Zedekiah, until the captivity. His ministry spans decades of decline. God set a faithful voice through the long, sad slide of Judah to exile.
'Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee... I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.' God chose him before birth. The LORD's purposes for His servants begin long before they are born.
'Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee... I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.' God chose him before birth. The LORD's purposes for His servants begin long before they are born.
Jeremiah pleads, 'I am a child,' but God answers, 'Be not afraid... for I am with thee to deliver thee.' He overrules the excuse. God's presence, not our ability, is the strength for His calling.
Jeremiah pleads, 'I am a child,' but God answers, 'Be not afraid... for I am with thee to deliver thee.' He overrules the excuse. God's presence, not our ability, is the strength for His calling.
Jeremiah pleads, 'I am a child,' but God answers, 'Be not afraid... for I am with thee to deliver thee.' He overrules the excuse. God's presence, not our ability, is the strength for His calling.

The LORD touches his mouth: 'I have put my words in thy mouth,' setting him over nations to root out and to plant. His message is God's own. The prophet speaks not his own thoughts but the word God gives.

The LORD touches his mouth: 'I have put my words in thy mouth,' setting him over nations to root out and to plant. His message is God's own. The prophet speaks not his own thoughts but the word God gives.
Shown an almond rod, Jeremiah hears, 'I will hasten my word to perform it.' The early-blooming branch signifies watchfulness. God is awake over His word, swift to fulfil what He has spoken.
Shown an almond rod, Jeremiah hears, 'I will hasten my word to perform it.' The early-blooming branch signifies watchfulness. God is awake over His word, swift to fulfil what He has spoken.

A boiling pot tilting from the north warns that 'out of the north an evil shall break forth' for their idolatry. Judgment is coming from Babylon. God explains the calamity as the fruit of forsaking Him.

A boiling pot tilting from the north warns that 'out of the north an evil shall break forth' for their idolatry. Judgment is coming from Babylon. God explains the calamity as the fruit of forsaking Him.

A boiling pot tilting from the north warns that 'out of the north an evil shall break forth' for their idolatry. Judgment is coming from Babylon. God explains the calamity as the fruit of forsaking Him.

A boiling pot tilting from the north warns that 'out of the north an evil shall break forth' for their idolatry. Judgment is coming from Babylon. God explains the calamity as the fruit of forsaking Him.

God bids him gird up and speak; though kings and people fight him, 'they shall not prevail... I am with thee.' He is made 'a defenced city.' The faithful servant is opposed but kept by the God who sent him.
